sections.paragraph:
ἴδιος, α, ον [opp. Hom. δήμιος ‘public’]

—1. ‘belonging to oneself’, (one’s) own, in some instances practically equivalent to a pers. pronoun

—a. w. emphasis on ownership Ac 4:32.

—b. w. focus on distinctiveness, peculiarity, or appropriateness Mt 22:5; 25:15; Lk 6:41; J 1:42; 8:44; 10:3f; 15:19; Ac 1:25; 2:8; Ro 10:3; 1 Cor 15:23, 38; Tit 1:3; 2:5, 9; 2 Pt 2:22. As noun: οἱ ἴδιοι own (people) J 13:1 (disciples); Ac 4:23; 24:23; 1 Ti 5:8 (kin); τὰ ἴδια home Lk 18:28; Ac 21:6; πράσσειν τὰ ἴδια attend to one’s own business 1 Th 4:11.

—2. special constructions ἰδίᾳ individually, respectively 1 Cor 12:11; ϰατ’ ἰδίαν apart, privately Mt 14:13, 23; 20:17; Mk 4:34; 6:31f; 9:2; Lk 10:23; Ac 23:19; Gal 2:2.
